title: AIMS event calendar
description: Schema reference for validate_calendar.py. Validation is implemented in Python rather than a JSON Schema library to avoid runtime dependencies. Calendars drive the deterministic 'Upcoming Events' report section (#91) and provide context for the AI qualitative layer (#92).

notes.files: data/calendars/macro_events.json holds central-bank and macro-release dates maintained by hand from officially published yearly schedules; data/calendars/earnings.json is refreshed from yfinance by update_calendars.py on a schedule.
notes.date: Event date (YYYY-MM-DD). For multi-day central-bank meetings, the decision/statement day is recorded.
notes.tagging: Events tag instruments via canonical_ids (specific instruments) and/or asset_classes (whole classes, e.g. an FOMC decision tagging equity_index, equity, and commodity). Every event must carry at least one tag.
notes.windowing: Report and Slack rendering shows events strictly after the analysis date within a configurable window (default 7 days), so output stays deterministic given the artifact date.
